title: Knowledge Graph
description: Knowledge Graph documents overview material for Knowledge Graph in the knowledge domain. It is classified as a concept page, so readers can understand its topic, scope, and place in the wiki taxonomy. The page highlights What a Knowledge Graph Is, Core Structure, What Knowledge Graphs Are Good At, Knowledge Graph Versus Ontology, and Practical Example, with examples, commands, diagrams, or references kept close to the relevant explanation. Important search terms include graph, ontology, entity, relation, and knowledge-graph, helping both humans and generated indexes connect this page with adjacent concepts. The route is knowledge / concepts / knowledge-graph, and the frontmatter keeps the canonical subject, ontology role, and navigation context aligned with repository validation.
